commands.hypothesis: Tests vel 5-10 SOL/min slice as a discrete velocity-band strategy. Hypothesis: tokens with steady-but-not-explosive bonding-curve fill velocity would have healthier post-graduation paths than the broader vel<20 universe.

commands.note: KILLED 2026-05-14 at n=86. Outcome: avg -1.82%, net -0.783 SOL, 67% SL exits (58/86). Vel 5-10 band did NOT meaningfully outperform broader universes; the strategy's biggest exit reason was hitting stop-loss before reaching TP. Cost model (gap penalties + round-trip slippage) eats the modest mean return. Lesson: a single velocity band in isolation is not enough — needs to be paired with a path-shape or concentration signal to clear the SOL bar.

commands.hypothesis: Mean ALL-population price path peaks at +3.9% near T+65 then bleeds to -4.6% by T+300 (price-path-stats.json). Hypothesis: tight TP=10 / SL=15 / 90s-hold on the unfiltered entry-gated population would harvest the mean reversion before the bleed sets in. Bet on early-exit + filter-free deployment to capture the highest possible sample n.

commands.note: KILLED 2026-05-14 at n=206 (largest sample of any deployment to date). Outcome: avg -1.18%, net -1.2197 SOL. 52% SL exits (108/206) vs 35% timeouts (71) and only 13% TP hits (27). Hypothesis falsified: the +3.9% mean-path peak at T+65 visible in price-path-stats is NOT capturable in live trading because (a) the gap-penalty cost model on tight TP/SL eats most of the per-trade margin, and (b) the few trades that DO hit TP get offset by the SL fills on the bottom 50% of the distribution. Filter-free + tight-exit is structurally negative-EV on memecoins. Lesson: do not deploy filter-free strategies even on apparent mean-reversion signals — the cost model demands selection, and unfiltered universes have catastrophic tails that wider TPs cannot compensate for.

commands.hypothesis: Vel 5-10 + max_drawdown_0_30 > -10% — combines the slow-velocity slice with a path-shape filter excluding deep pre-entry drawdowns. Hypothesis: slow steady graduation + healthy 0-30s price action would lift the vel 5-10 band into profitability via tail-trimming.

commands.note: KILLED 2026-05-14 at n=57. Outcome: avg -5.4%, net -1.5399 SOL, 61% SL exits (35/57). Even with the dd>-10% tail-trim filter, the vel 5-10 band failed to clear costs. Confirms the v9shadow-vel5-10 kill conclusion: the slow-velocity slice is fundamentally lower-EV than the vel 20-50 band (which IS positive — see v20/v23 baseline). Don't keep deploying vel 5-10 variants — the band itself is the problem, not the modifier filters stacked on top.
